home.title: Zenjo
home.subtitle: Mahjong Solitaire
home.description: A beautifully crafted Mahjong Solitaire experience that blends classic tile-matching with modern puzzle mechanics. Tap, match, and clear your way through 20 handcrafted levels across 4 thematic chapters.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: Zenjo: Mahjong Solitaire
privacy.intro: Yasin Cetin ("we", "our", or "us") built Zenjo: Mahjong Solitaire ("the App") as a free, ad-supported iOS application. This page explains what information the App handles, how it is used, and the choices you have.
privacy.informationWeCollectText: The App does not require you to sign in or provide any personal information. You can set a local username and pick an avatar, but these are stored only on your device and are never uploaded to us.
privacy.informationWeCollectText2: The App does use a small number of third-party services that automatically collect technical or gameplay data, as described below.
privacy.deviceIdentifiersText: The App does not ask for App Tracking Transparency permission and does not track you across apps or websites. Google AdMob uses Apple's SKAdNetwork for privacy-preserving ad attribution and may use non-identifying device signals (such as the app's vendor identifier — IDFV) to serve contextual or limited-personalization ads. No advertising identifier (IDFA) is read without your explicit system-level consent.
privacy.analyticsDataText: We use Firebase Analytics to collect anonymous gameplay and usage events, including:
analyticsEvents: Game lifecycle events (game_start, game_complete, game_pause, game_resume, game_restart)
Gameplay actions (tile_selected, tile_matched, hint_used, shuffle_used, undo_used, continue_after_ad)
Settings and preferences (setting_changed, theme_changed, onboarding_completed, progress_reset)
privacy.analyticsPropsText: We also set the following aggregate user properties so we can understand how players progress, all derived from on-device state:
analyticsProps: Current daily streak and best streak
privacy.analyticsDataText2: This data is aggregated and anonymous. It helps us understand how players interact with the App so we can improve the experience.
privacy.crashDiagnosticsText: We use Firebase Crashlytics to collect crash reports, non-fatal errors, and diagnostic context when something goes wrong. Alongside stack traces and device state, we attach small gameplay context keys to help reproduce issues:
crashKeys: Level ID, tiles remaining, and current score
Bar slot count and current game phase (playing, paused, won, lost)
Breadcrumb logs of recent gameplay actions
privacy.pushNotificationsText: If you grant notification permission (we only ask after you finish your first level), the App registers a device token with Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) so we can send optional re-engagement and streak reminders. The device is subscribed to one or more of the following topics based on your play state:
fcmTopics: all_users — every player who has enabled notifications
streak_active — players with an ongoing daily streak
streak_lapsed — players whose streak has recently ended
privacy.remoteConfigText: The App uses Firebase Remote Config to fetch small configuration values (such as the support URL, privacy URL, and App Store URL) from Firebase. Remote Config is read-only from your device's perspective — no personal or gameplay data is sent.
privacy.performanceText: We use Firebase Performance Monitoring to collect anonymized metrics such as app startup time and network request latency. This helps us identify slow or broken code paths.
howWeUseItems: To serve advertisements through Google AdMob
To analyze app usage and improve the game experience
To identify and fix crashes and technical issues
To monitor app performance and reliability
To send optional re-engagement and streak notifications (only if you opt in)
To remotely configure non-sensitive values like support URLs and feature flags
privacy.thirdPartyText: The App uses the following third-party services, each governed by its own privacy policy:
privacy.dataRetentionText: Analytics data is retained in Firebase for up to 14 months. Crash reports are retained for up to 90 days. Advertising data is managed by Google according to their own policies. FCM device tokens are retained only while the App is installed and notifications are enabled; uninstalling the App invalidates the token. Remote Config stores no user data.
privacy.dataSharingText: We do not sell, trade, or transfer your data to third parties beyond the services listed above. Data collected by these SDKs is governed by their respective privacy policies. Your game progress, profile, and statistics live only on your device — we have no cloud save or server-side account system.
privacy.childrensPrivacyText: The App is rated 4+ and is suitable for all ages. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13, and the App complies with the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA). If you believe we have inadvertently collected information from a child, please contact us and we will promptly delete it.
yourRightsItems: Disable notifications at any time in iOS Settings → Notifications → Zenjo
Reset your advertising identifier via iOS Settings → Privacy & Security → Apple Advertising
Reset all locally stored progress, stats, and profile from the App's Settings → Reset Progress
Request deletion of any Firebase data associated with your device by contacting us at the email below
privacy.changesText: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated effective date. We encourage you to review this page periodically.
privacy.contactUsText: If you have questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at:

support.title: Support
support.subtitle: Zenjo: Mahjong Solitaire
support.contactPrompt: Need help? Have feedback? Reach out to us:

faqs.a: Yes! Every board is generated using a reverse-build algorithm that guarantees a solution exists. No luck needed — just strategy and skill.

faqs.q: What are hints, shuffle, and undo?
faqs.a: These are power-ups to help you when you're stuck. Hints highlight a matching tile for the one you've selected — you start each level with 3. Shuffle randomizes tile positions and you get 1 per level. Undo reverses your last move — you start with 3. When you run out, you can watch a short rewarded ad to earn more.

faqs.q: How does the combo system work?
faqs.a: Match tiles quickly in succession to build your combo multiplier (up to 3x). The faster you match, the higher your score. Tapping a blocked tile or using a power-up resets the combo.

faqs.q: How do I earn 3 stars on a level?
faqs.a: Stars are based on your score and completion time. To earn 3 stars, you need to hit the score target and finish within the time limit. Chaining combos and using as few power-ups as possible will push your score up.

faqs.q: How do I resume a game I was playing?
faqs.a: If you close the app mid-level, a Continue Game option appears on the home screen so you can pick up exactly where you left off.
Zenjo tracks a daily play streak — play at least once a day to extend it. Missing a day resets your current streak, but your best streak is saved. You can see both in your profile.

faqs.q: Can I change my username or avatar?
faqs.a: Yes. Open Settings → Profile to change your username and pick from 8 avatars: Dragon, Samurai, Phoenix, Sage, Bonsai, Mandala, Tiger, or Yin-Yang.

faqs.q: How do I change tile or background themes?
faqs.a: Open Settings → Themes to switch between available tile sets and board backgrounds.

faqs.q: How do I reset my progress?
faqs.a: In the app, go to Settings and scroll down to Reset Progress. This action cannot be undone — all stars, scores, level progress, streaks, statistics, and profile data stored on the device will be permanently deleted.

faqs.q: My game data was lost. Can I recover it?
faqs.a: Zenjo stores all progress locally on your device — there is no cloud save or sign-in. If you delete the app or reset your device, the data cannot be recovered. We recommend keeping the app installed to preserve your progress.
Zenjo is currently available on iOS only (iOS 18 or later). There is no Android version at this time.
Yes. Core gameplay works fully offline. An internet connection is only needed for watching rewarded ads, receiving push notifications, and syncing anonymous analytics.

faqs.q: Why does the game sometimes not show an ad after a level?
faqs.a: Ad frequency is deliberately limited so ads stay unobtrusive: no interstitials during the tutorial (levels 1–3), roughly one every 3 levels afterward, a maximum of 3 per app session, and at least 180 seconds between interstitials. Rewarded ads (for hints, shuffles, undos, and continues) are always on demand.

faqs.q: How do I turn off notifications?
faqs.a: You'll be asked for notification permission after you finish your first level. To change it later, open iOS Settings → Notifications → Zenjo and toggle Allow Notifications.
